title: Pan-Fried Paneer Halloumi-Style Salad

ingredients.name: paneer, sliced 1 cm thick
visual_checks: firm commercial paneer works best; homemade may crumble
ingredients.notes: firm commercial paneer works best; homemade may crumble

subs.name: firm tofu, pressed and dried, for a vegan version
subs.notes: sears equally well if dried thoroughly

subs.name: any neutral oil for frying; use oil for the dressing too if olive oil is unavailable
subs.notes: olive oil adds a Mediterranean note

subs.name: any neutral oil for frying; use oil for the dressing too if olive oil is unavailable
subs.notes: olive oil adds a Mediterranean note

subs.name: a squeeze of extra lemon juice
subs.notes: amchur carries a fruity sourness lemon alone lacks

steps.text: Pat the paneer slices thoroughly dry with paper towels — surface moisture is the enemy of a good sear. Season each slice with a little black pepper and salt on both sides.

Heat a dry skillet or tawa on high flame until very hot. Add just 1 tablespoon olive oil. Place paneer slices without crowding the pan and cook for 2–3 minutes without moving until the underside is deep golden and releases cleanly.

Flip each slice and cook for another 2 minutes until the second side is equally golden. Remove to a plate. The paneer should be firm, golden and slightly chewy on the outside — reminiscent of grilled halloumi.
Toss the tomatoes, cucumber, spring onion and mint in a bowl. Drizzle over lemon juice and 2 tablespoons olive oil, season with salt and amchur. Toss gently.

Arrange the salad on a plate, lay the warm golden paneer slices on top, scatter dried oregano if using, and serve immediately. The contrast between the warm seared paneer and cool crisp salad is the point of the dish.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: paneer → firm tofu, pressed and dried, for a vegan version (sears equally well if dried thoroughly); olive-oil → any neutral oil for frying; use oil for the dressing too if olive oil is unavailable (olive oil adds a Mediterranean note); amchur → a squeeze of extra lemon juice (amchur carries a fruity sourness lemon alone lacks)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Paneer is widely available across India and behaves exactly like halloumi when seared — it holds shape, browns beautifully and becomes pleasantly chewy. Use a well-seasoned tawa on high flame. Amchur powder substitutes perfectly for sumac in the dressing.
